name: 46 · Live Video Stream (MSE <video> render adapter)
description:
The VIDEO leg of the streaming arc — what makes the original goal ("streaming load-bearing for video") concrete. Sibling of demo 45 (fragmented MP4 audio); same shape, only the codec differs. The producer synthesizes a 3 s 320×240 clip (scrolling gradient + bouncing box) and muxes it with PyAV/libx264 into a FRAGMENTED MP4 / H.264 byte stream (`ftyp` + `moov`(mvex) init segment, then `moof` + `mdat` media fragments), pacing the fragments over the Data/Out channel `video` in ~real time. The element content_type is `video/mp4;codecs="avc1.42E01E"`, so the UI render-adapter registry (planLiveRender) returns mediaKind=video and the panel appends the stream into a `<video>` element via Media Source Extensions — the same descriptor-driven presentation dispatch as the audio path, exercising the video branch.

A validator drains the channel transport-unaware (`for chunk in stream('video')`) and parses the received bytes' MP4 box structure to assert they are a genuine, multi-fragment, MSE-appendable stream — the wire-level half of a verification the browser can't automate. JetStream is durable + ordered, so the received byte count equals the produced one. PyAV bundles libav with libx264, so no system ffmpeg is needed.

Start → producer (PyAV H.264 fragmented MP4 over `video`) → validator (parse boxes off the wire) → End. Run: `mekhan run 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000460 --start-tokens '[{"start_block_id":"start","token":{}}]'`. To watch it: while the instance runs, open it in the app, find the `video` channel, and hit Play live — the clip decodes and plays in a `<video>` element as the fragments land.
